figures.caption: Figure 1: Computational domain Ω = [0, 2]2 m2. Thermal conductivity field (left) and volumetric heat capacity field (right).

Figure 2: Thermal graph on the computational domain. Boundary nodes (1–4) handle radiative exchange with deep space; internal nodes (5–8) correspond to sensor locations within the monitored subdomains. Green edges denote conductive couplings.
Table 2: Calibration results for the internal heater line configuration.
Table 3: Calibration results for the benchmark configuration.
Figure 3: Predicted (orange) and reference (blue) temperature trajectories for the external source configuration.
Figure 4: Predicted (orange) and reference (blue) temperature trajectories for the internal heater line configuration.
Figure 5: Predicted (orange) and reference (blue) temperature trajectories for the benchmark test, combining external and internal forcings.
Figure 6: Training diagnostics for the benchmark configuration.

Figure 7: Effect of training window length on the predicted temperature trajectories. Each panel corresponds to a different value of Tb, with background shading identifying training (green), validation (yellow), and test (red) segments.

Figure 8: Noise robustness results. Top: predicted (orange) vs. reference (blue) trajectories under 1% (left) and 5% (right) measurement noise. Bottom: box plots of windowed test errors for each noise level, confirming the absence of error accumulation over long horizons.
